source_display: AI as Brain Crutches for ADHD
evidence: Vrijen shared his personal interest in building AI tools as 'brain crutches' for ADHD—filling gaps between therapy, medication, and coaching

source_display: Gap Between Therapy/Medication/Coaching in EF Support
evidence: Chris building 360 product specifically to address executive function needs—tools for supporters, teachers, and individuals with EF needs
Chris revealed he's been building his edtech product for almost a year, leveraging AI tools

Being a member of the audience that I'm serving... I have ADHD. I was a teacher... I can validate my own internal feelings about something with other people who are more often than not sharing a similar experience
I think they're not, they're not worth your time, my friend - Strong reaction when V warned about investors. He doesn't want VC money.
We have a very large email list that we've built over the years. It's up to 65,000 active email subscribers. Feel very proud of that.

source_display: Design Partner vs Feature-Creep Predator Distinction
evidence: V provided strategic framing on pilot deals - distinguishing design partners from feature creep predators
The core concept is a 'marketplace of all possible futures' - allowing users to 'jump into' career simulations mapped to real jobs and companies
Maximilian shared that he's preparing to launch his new product in approximately one month
He explained that jobs are essentially 'getting paid to learn,' but gatekeeping makes experience inaccessible
You can fail as many times as you want and then you can restart over. You can just reset at the click of a button.
HackerRank... really robust assessment tool I think is a piece of shit service because it just doesn't baseline or ground the value on anything
The only thing that businesses care about is if you can add value to whatever they're making - portfolio-first, assessment-second positioning is intentional
Whatever you do on your work has to be some sort of visible link between you and the service or product that's being sold. Because otherwise, like what?
Aim to sell Careerspan towards the middle of 2026, while using the next few months to pursue revenue generation opportunities to improve valuation
Team consensus is to combine both strategies (sale + revenue) rather than choosing one exclusively - will pursue revenue opportunities while maintaining active sale conversations
Vrijen introduced context engineering as the foundational practice: not prompting the AI directly, but cultivating the context it uses to generate desired outputs
He outlined three strategic levels: conversation-level, environment-level, and pipeline-level context engineering

source_display: AI Productivity as Mental Model Problem
evidence: Vrijen argued the core issue isn't a technology or tactics problem but a mental model problem—users collect techniques without understanding how they integrate into effective systems
you can unhobble your AI a lot when you give it access to your files and data... when you give the AI its own home
Ben Guo (co-founder of Zo Computer) presented Zo's managed cloud AI environment
job security is dead and you need to be building career security for yourself... it has a lot to do with being findable and being visible
Logan conducted deep LinkedIn data export analysis (5,000 messages, 18 years, 48 CSV files) generating 10+ experiments including network clusters and opportunity identification
come up with like two to three things that would really delight me... what's LinkedIn sitting on that they should be showing us

source_display: Exploiting Passive Moments with Mobile AI
evidence: I was traveling to San Francisco yesterday and I was just doing this while I was waiting in line at the airport - exploiting AI integration with mobile channels to work in passive moments
Logan Currie (Careerspan co-founder and AI productivity nerd) presented alongside Vrijen
